plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad index concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL as they are flagged for exit or are leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single stock risk.
Maintain a cash buffer of 15-20% for capital preservation.
Focus on top-quality names and broad index exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect limited upside potential in exchange for enhanced stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Reduce QQQI to 10% to balance income with growth.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL due to flags or leverage.
Add to CRWD, an uptrending quality tech name, to 15%.
Buy XLF for 5% to diversify into a strong sector.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer, keeping total adds <= total trims + buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index, quality tech, and some diversification. Expect moderate drawdowns with reasonable growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell QQQI and QQQ to free capital for higher growth.
Exit SMH and SPAL due to flags or below-stop status.
Add to CRWD, pressing this uptrending quality tech name to 20%.
Re-add SOXL to 10% for aggressive semiconductor exposure.
Buy AMD for 10%, a high-scoring chip setup from the scanner.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by stretching growth and leverage. It leans into high-scoring setups and leveraged ETFs, which amplify both gains and losses. Be prepared for significantly higher volatility and potential drawdowns, as leveraged ETFs decay daily.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate to income.
Maximize QQQI to 30% for steady monthly distributions.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to reduce growth and leverage.
Buy XLF for 10% to add a stable income-oriented sector.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on steady monthly distributions. It rotates into income/covered-call ETFs and broad index, cutting leverage and high-beta names. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
